analytical_findings.label: REAL
analytical_findings.hypothesis: Target and judge share a vendor; judge's biases overlap with the target, flattering same-vendor responses.
analytical_findings.note: Target and judge are identical: openai/gpt-4o-mini. Judge will share the target's distributional biases.
analytical_findings.remediation: Use a different vendor or stronger model for --judge-*.

analytical_findings.label: REAL
analytical_findings.hypothesis: Dataset is small enough that Pearson KC-4 has no statistical power; correlation threshold becomes a random pass/fail.
analytical_findings.note: Test slice has 5 items. Pearson correlation at n=5 has weak statistical power; KC-4 pass/fail may be random.
analytical_findings.remediation: Expand test set to at least 20 items, or raise --min-kc4 adaptively (handled by AdaptationPlan).

analytical_findings.label: NEW
analytical_findings.hypothesis: System prompt variants are too similar; sensitivity on the system_prompt_variant axis will be artificially low.
analytical_findings.note: All 2 system prompts have near-identical length (21-29 chars); they may be too similar to produce meaningful sensitivity.
analytical_findings.remediation: Author variants that differ in role framing, not just wording; vary length by at least 2x where possible.

analytical_findings.label: REAL
analytical_findings.hypothesis: A single rubric dimension carries the majority of the weight; judge noise on that one dimension dominates the fitness.
analytical_findings.note: Dimension 'accuracy' carries 85% of the rubric weight; judge noise on that single dimension will dominate fitness.
analytical_findings.remediation: Rebalance rubric so no single dimension exceeds ~50% weight, or explicitly declare this concentration is intentional.

analytical_findings.label: GHOST
analytical_findings.hypothesis: Judge output budget is SMALL but rubric has many dimensions + gates; judge response may be truncated before scoring all axes.
analytical_findings.note: Judge budget small adequate for 3 axes.

analytical_findings.label: NEW
analytical_findings.hypothesis: Dataset items have no reference text while the rubric's dimension descriptions imply comparison to a ground truth.
analytical_findings.note: No dataset item has a reference field; judge scores purely by rubric without ground-truth anchor.
analytical_findings.remediation: If your rubric's descriptions imply comparison to a reference, add reference fields or reword the rubric to be self-contained.

analytical_findings.label: GHOST
analytical_findings.hypothesis: User did not pass --test; walk-forward cannot run; ship decision has no generalisation evidence.
analytical_findings.note: Held-out slice provided; walk-forward will run.
